goal: Ship v6.0.1: close the two trust-chain holes the Fable/xhigh fifth review found in v6.0.0 — review-freshness now treats an empty post-merge diff as N/A not stale (the shipped tag was failing its own verify), and security_review.diff_sha256 is now validated (was independent_review only, so a security approval survived later code changes at the risk boundary) — plus the Codex Stop timeout, the config-without-task first-contact trap, and malformed-input crash hardening. Closes the loop this time.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All core gate-case suites pass after every change, including the new commit-first-evasion, honest-committer, object-AC, blocked-row, test-shrinkage, and protect-harness cases
Diff-grounded reality gates (scope integrity case/glob fixes, bugfix word-boundary keywords, merge-base default, render-prompt) hold under their extended suite
Hook shims fail truthfully (no fabricated secret denials, sys.executable, record-deleted block, anchored destructive patterns) under the extended hook suite
Memory is read-only on recall, pooled without the 60/40 tax, provenance-stamped, and surfaces outcomes; recall_budget_chars is honored
Heterogeneity degrades loudly (SKIPPED line, model_routing.families override, as_of staleness) and setup-models refuses placeholder ids
Control plane joins delegations by session_id, counts unjoinable rows, represents droid runs as events, exposes drift canaries, prunes all tables, and ships only with --with-control-plane
Static task-classing, right-size, and SKILL-truth lint cases pass, including the two new v6 pins
The npm funnel works end to end: parser does not swallow subcommands, manifest-driven check, init->check->remove leaves a clean tree, next steps exit 0
The regenerated bench fixture passes the cost-fields validator that CI now runs on every committed results file
The example orchestration config stays valid and check-config prints the loud heterogeneity status line

constraints: net negative lines: deletions must pay for every addition
every behavior change lands with eval coverage in the repo's own suites
no gate may depend on the opt-in control plane
breaking changes documented in CHANGELOG 6.0.0 (object ACs at medium+, 4-value verdict enum, merge-base --base default, control plane opt-in, read-only recall)
non_goals: Wave 4.3 live ablation runs (pre-registered in bench/PROTOCOL.md, next milestone)
task-class collapse 4->2, reviewer-model binding, second reviewer (deferred pending ablation data)
mutation-testing engine (the test-shrinkage counter is the 30-line version)
assumptions: v6.0.0 is a breaking release; older records with string ACs at medium+ must be upgraded or re-tiered
the same-model orchestrator (Claude) delegates implementation to Claude workers; independent review routed cross-family to a GPT-5-class Codex reviewer per stock routing
verification_plan: all seven eval suites + static cases green on the combined tree after each phase
live scratch-dir init/check/remove cycle per host
verify umbrella (record gates + diff audit + evidence re-execution + AC coverage) against merge-base
fresh-context cross-family review + security review on the full diff, then attest-review

minimality_decision.reason: New code concentrated in: test-shrinkage counter (~30 lines), render-prompt (~60), install manifest/uninstall, outcome/provenance rows, families map. Every addition paid for by deletions; repo net -2,205 lines vs main. Deletion applied first wherever possible; reuse applied before any new code (verify umbrella at Stop, _resolve_base for merge-base, existing deny path, control-plane token parsers).

rejected_lower_rungs.why: the deep review verified live enforcement holes (fabricated evidence clears the Stop gate; commit-first evasion) — no change leaves the core promise untrue
applied throughout (5 templates, 12 bench stubs, 3 protocol docs, tool shapes, 60/40 split, fuzzy matcher, dead probe) but deletion alone cannot close enforcement gaps
applied first for every new behavior; the residual new code is what the gates required
already stdlib-only; the new gates are stdlib code — this rung is the ceiling we stayed under, not an alternative
no host-native feature enforces record gates or diff-grounded checks
zero runtime dependencies is a hard constraint
a multi-gap trust-chain release cannot be a one-liner

plan: S1 trust chain + funnel: gate engine, hooks, installer/npm, control plane (4 parallel workers, disjoint files)
S2 shrink + single-source: templates/schema/prompts, SKILL+references, memory+routing, bench (4 parallel workers) + orchestrator wiring
S3 docs truth pass: README/CHANGELOG/ROADMAP/quickstart/count lint/version sync
S4 dogfood + review: this record, cross-family review, security review, attest, release
verification: per-slice all suites green before commit; final verify umbrella vs merge-base + fresh-context reviews + attest-review
rollback: git revert the release-train merge on main; deleting the GitHub release/tag halts npm publish; no data migrations (control DB rebuilds, schema bump exports events first)

commands_run.evidence: 50/50 eval cases passed (44 pre-release + object-AC, blocked-row, commit-first, honest-committer, reviewer-contract-drift, paper-trail lint cases)
33/33 reality eval cases passed (23 pre-release + scope-case, glob-tightening, bugfix word-boundary, test-shrinkage, under-fanning, render-prompt, timeout cases)
30/30 hook eval cases passed (16 pre-release + truthful-failure, protect-harness, record-deleted, destructive-pattern FP/FN cases)
32/32 memory eval cases passed (26 pre-release + read-only recall, one-pool, outcome, provenance, staleness, budget-wiring cases)
29/29 routing eval cases passed (24 pre-release + heterogeneity-status, families-override, as_of, placeholder-guard cases)
35/35 control-plane add-on cases passed (27 pre-release + session_id join, unjoinable counting, one-to-one join, droid events, zero-usage canary, retention, opt-in-install cases)
19/19 static cases passed (11 pre-release + string-AC-blocked/pass, blocked-reason, e2e-evidence, proving-path-no-floor, SKILL-truth pins)
30/30 node tests passed incl. init->check->remove clean-tree integration and the --dry-run-swallows-subcommand parser case
OK: 12 run(s) carry required cost fields — the committed fixture passes the validator CI now runs on every bench/results/*.json
config ok, preceded by the loud heterogeneity status line (SKIPPED — placeholder models in the example config, named explicitly instead of silent)
41/41 hook cases incl. protect_harness deny/allow, destructive-wrapper FP/FN, truthful scan-text failure, record-deletion block

models_used.notes: 8 parallel fresh-context workers across S1+S2 + 1 docs worker in S3, disjoint file ownership
cross-family fresh-context review via the Codex runtime
fresh-context security pass, separate session from all implementers

findings.detail: Both v6.0.0 majors reproduced-then-fixed (empty-diff freshness N/A, security_review freshness); 7 adversarial probes; all 8 suites green. Two minors fixed in follow-up (sibling plan crash, codex timeout pin).

security_review freshness now validated (the fix under review); empty-diff N/A path confirmed non-abusable (phantom completion holds at terminal status, auto-base prevents commit-first emptying). Stop-gate narrowing reasoned acceptable.

open_risks: Wave 4.3 live ablation still outstanding (bench/PROTOCOL.md pre-registered).
Documented residual limits unchanged (Stop latency budget, Bash-mediated tamper, ran_checks warn-not-fail).

validation_contract.goal: Ship v6.0.0: close the enforcement gaps the 2026-07-20 deep review verified (verify umbrella at the Stop gate, merge-base diff grounding, object ACs at medium+, satisfiable blocked rows, test-shrinkage and under-fanning gates, protect-harness guard, truthful hook failures), repair the adoption funnel (install manifest, host-aware check, uninstall, codex AGENTS.md, render-prompt), shrink the surface (4-artifact paper trail, 4-value verdict enum, control plane demoted to opt-in add-on, dead templates and speculative tool shapes deleted), make memory read-only-on-recall with outcome feedback, and pre-register the bench protocol with forced decision rules — net negative lines, per docs/improvement-plan-2026-07-20.md

callers_checked: stop_gate -> verify umbrella (child invocation contract: exit codes only, no stdout-prefix parsing)
pretooluse_guard -> scan-text (exit-code discrimination: findings vs broken runtime)
install.py <- packages/npm/src/install.mjs (manifest is the shared contract)
repo_map.notes: Release-wide diff; context_map.files uses explicit per-area globs (the intended escape hatch for a repo-spanning release), not single-file mappings.

completion_record.goal: Ship v6.0.1: close the two trust-chain holes the Fable/xhigh fifth review found in v6.0.0 — review-freshness now treats an empty post-merge diff as N/A not stale (the shipped tag was failing its own verify), and security_review.diff_sha256 is now validated (was independent_review only, so a security approval survived later code changes at the risk boundary) — plus the Codex Stop timeout, the config-without-task first-contact trap, and malformed-input crash hardening. Closes the loop this time.

bugfix_test_waiver: Regression tests DID ship with this fix as eval cases under evals/ — the bugfix-test co-presence heuristic's TEST_PATH_MARKERS (test/spec/__tests__) does not recognize CQL's eval-case test location. New/reworked cases: case_empty_diff_freshness_is_na and case_security_review_freshness_checked (evals/run_reality_evals.py) pin the two majors; case_stop_gate_allows_config_without_task + tombstone rework (evals/run_hook_evals.py) pin the stop-gate change. Per CONTRIBUTING.md, eval cases are this repo's regression tests.
